sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: A collection contains multiple objects of the same type. Kotlin provides three primary collection types: List, Set, and Map.

sections.heading: Immutable vs Mutable
sections.content: Kotlin distinguishes between read-only (immutable) and mutable collections.

sections.heading: Collection Mutability
items: Immutable (Read-Only): Data can be accessed but not modified. Created using functions like listOf().
Mutable: Data can be added, removed, or updated. Created using functions like mutableListOf().

sections.heading: Lists
sections.content: A List is an ordered collection. Elements are accessed by index (starting from 0), and duplicates are allowed.
sections.code:
val fruits = listOf("Apple", "Banana", "Cherry")
println(fruits[1])

val animals = mutableListOf("Lion", "Tiger")
animals.add("Bear")
animals.removeAt(0)

sections.heading: Sets
sections.content: A Set contains unique elements only. Duplicate values are automatically ignored.
sections.code:
val numbers = setOf(1, 2, 3, 3, 3)
println(numbers.size)

val mutableSet = mutableSetOf("A", "B")
mutableSet.add("A")

sections.heading: Maps
sections.content: A Map stores key-value pairs. Keys must be unique, but values can be duplicated.
sections.code:
val airPorts = mapOf("LHR" to "London", "JFK" to "New York")

val userScores = mutableMapOf("Alex" to 10, "Samy" to 20)
userScores["Alex"] = 15
userScores["John"] = 5

sections.heading: Common Collection Operations
sections.content: Kotlin provides functional operators to transform and filter collections without manual loops.

rows.description: Returns true if at least one element matches.
Returns the number of matching elements.
Returns first element or null if empty.

sections.heading: Functional Operations Example
sections.content: These operators allow expressive, concise transformations without explicit loops.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Prefer read-only collections unless modification is required.
Use functional operators like filter and map for cleaner code.
